justification: Both models are equally incorrect on material, monolayer indicators, and Raman physics for WSe₂; neither is closer to ground truth. B notes the Si peak correctly but this does not outweigh the total absence of TMD reasoning in either output.

step1.A: Failed - no WSe₂, wrong HgS assignment, missed all monolayer indicators
step1.B: Failed - no WSe₂, mislabeled as Si, ignored TMD signatures

step2.A: Poor, hallucinates irrelevant HgS modes and PL without TMD context
step2.B: Poor, focuses only on Si modes and anomalies without 2D-material analysis

step3.A: Structurally complete for wrong material; no WSe₂ evidence listed
step3.B: Structurally complete for wrong material; no WSe₂ evidence listed

thinking: Both candidates completely failed Step 1 by not identifying WSe₂ at all (A chose cinnabar HgS for the ~248 cm⁻¹ peak; B chose silicon while calling the same peak anomalous). Neither referenced the merged E¹₂g/A₁g, enhanced 2LA(M) at ~260 cm⁻¹, B¹₂g absence, or any monolayer TMD diagnostics, and both ignored the Si substrate peak's expected presence. Their chain-of-thought never engages WSe₂ physics or confounds, making reasoning quality equally poor and the classification results wholly incorrect.
